title: pending_proposals.json has 3 RMW writers, no cross-process lock, and a SHARED tmp path — atomicity guarantee is conditional on telegram being off
evidence: Three independent writers of /dispatch_state/pending_proposals.json, none take an flock (contrast orders_state.json which uses a .lock + fcntl.LOCK_EX): (1) pending_proposals_store.save (LIVE, ENABLE_PENDING_PROPOSALS_WRITE=True) does load->sweep->merge->save with `tmp = f"{path}.tmp"` then os.replace (lines 46-51); (2) telegram_approver.save_pending uses the IDENTICAL `tmp = f"{path}.tmp"` (line 1761) then os.replace — same tmp filename as the store; (3) postpone_sweeper.run_once does `pending=_load_json_safe(...); pending[oid]={...}; _atomic_write_json(PENDING_PROPOSALS_PATH, pending)` (lines 150-158, RMW, unique NamedTemporaryFile). os.replace makes each individual write atomic against torn READS, but it does NOT serialize concurrent read-modify-write across processes → classic lost-update; worse, writers (1) and (2) share the same `pending_proposals.json.tmp` path so two concurrent writers can truncate/torn each other's tmp before os.replace, defeating the atomic-rename guarantee entirely. The store docstring (lines 13-14) asserts safety: 'Pisarz: shadow_dispatcher (jedyny po wyłączeniu Telegrama/postpone -> brak wyścigu pisarzy; os.replace atomowy)'. That invariant is enforced only by external service state, not code.
impact: Currently safe (dispatch-telegram inactive => shadow is sole active writer; postpone-sweeper.timer is active but postponed_proposals.json is empty so it never reaches the pending write). If telegram is re-enabled, shadow + telegram concurrently RMW the same file via the same tmp path => lost proposal updates and/or a torn pending_proposals.json being os.replace'd into place, read by resweep / panel_watcher / console overlay.
protocol_note: BEFORE re-enabling dispatch-telegram (currently muted per MEMORY 26.06), the pending_proposals.json writers MUST be unified behind a single fcntl lock (mirror state_machine._locked_write) OR a single writer. Do NOT trust the pending_proposals_store docstring's 'brak wyscigu pisarzy' claim — it is true only while telegram+postpone are inert. The shared `{path}.tmp` filename between pending_proposals_store.save and telegram_approver.save_pending is the sharpest hazard (give each a unique tmp like postpone_sweeper does, and add a lock).
recommendation: Verify dispatch-telegram is still inactive; if a session re-enables it, first add cross-process locking (flock on a pending_proposals.json.lock) around all three writers and give save_pending a unique tmp name. Re-run grep for any 4th writer before flipping.

verification.reasoning: Every code-level claim verified true. pending_proposals_store.save (line 46) and telegram_approver.save_pending (line 1761) both literally use tmp=f"{path}.tmp" against the same PENDING_PATH (store line 29, telegram line 147), so writers 1 and 2 share the exact tmp file with no flock. postpone_sweeper._atomic_write_json (line 36) uses a unique NamedTemporaryFile, so it can only lost-update, never torn-replace, exactly as the finding itself states. The repo DOES have cross-process lock infrastructure used elsewhere (core/flags_io.py:69-72 LOCK_EX, core/jsonl_appender.py LOCK_EX), so the contrast is fair. Live state confirms currently safe: dispatch-shadow active and sole writer (log shows PENDING_PROPOSALS_WRITE upserted=1 each tick, flag ENABLE_PENDING_PROPOSALS_WRITE effectively ON in-process), dispatch-telegram dead, postpone timer active but postponed_proposals.json is 2 bytes empty so it never reaches the pending write. I downgrade P2 to P3 because: (1) torn-replace requires telegram+shadow co-writing, which violates the DOCUMENTED mutual-exclusion design: pending_proposals_store (Opcja B, 2026-06-26) was built to REPLACE telegram as sole writer after telegram was intentionally muted; the store docstring asserts telegram-off as the precondition. Re-enabling telegram concurrently is a config footgun, not a live bug. (2) The only telegram-independent race (shadow vs postpone) uses unique tmp so no torn read, only a dormant sub-second lost-update window (postponed empty). (3) Self-healing: shadow rewrites the FULL pending dict every tick (upsert_proposals load-sweep-merge-save), so any inconsistency self-corrects within ~2 min; pending_proposals.json is read by panel_watcher/resweep/console overlay, it is not a scoring/feasibility decision variable, so no durable or decision-path impact. Genuine but latent, conditional, and self-healing, worth a cheap hardening (unique tmp + flock) but not P2.

title: postpone_sweeper reads orders_state with wrong nesting ('orders') and wrong courier field ('cid') — manual-assignment resolution is dead
evidence: Line 102: `orders_state = state_machine._read_state()` returns the FLAT dict {order_id: record} (verified on disk: top-level len=364, NO 'orders' key, sample keys are order_ids). Line 103: `current = orders_state.get("orders", {}).get(oid) or {}` — there is no top-level 'orders' key, so this is ALWAYS {}. Line 105: `current_cid = current.get("cid")` — records carry `courier_id`, not `cid` (verified on disk: 0/364 records have 'cid', 364/364 have 'courier_id'). So current_cid is always None and the `if current_cid not in (None,...)` POSTPONE_RESOLVED branch (lines 105-109) is unreachable. git blame: the wrong nesting+field date to b78e31d3 (2026-05-14); commit 4c3c140 (2026-06-15) fixed only the call `load_state`->`_read_state()` on line 102 but left the downstream `.get('orders')`/`cid` consumer wrong — a partial fix.
impact: When an order is postponed and a coordinator then manually assigns it, the sweeper cannot detect the assignment (resolved path dead). On window expiry it instead re-runs assess_order and writes a fresh pending_proposals.json entry (line 151-158) for an already-assigned order => duplicate/phantom proposal, or wrongly escalates to KOORD. Also `current.get('raw')` order_event source is always empty, and `if not current` (always truthy-empty) skews the DROP_STALE branch. Dormant now (postponed_proposals.json empty because telegram off), but live the moment telegram/postpone is re-enabled.
protocol_note: postpone_sweeper.py:103/105 must use `orders_state.get(oid)` and `current.get('courier_id')` to match the flat orders_state schema (state_machine.get_order semantics). This is a known anti-pattern instance: a read-call was fixed (4c3c140) without fixing the consumer's assumed schema. Any future postpone re-activation must fix this first or postponed-then-manually-assigned orders will be re-proposed.
recommendation: Confirm postponed_proposals.json is empty and telegram inactive (dormant). If re-enabling postpone/telegram, fix the nesting/field first and add a test that a postponed order with courier_id set hits POSTPONE_RESOLVED. Cross-check state_machine.get_order for the canonical accessor.

verification.reasoning: All claims hold against code + disk + blame. (1) state_machine._read_state() (state_machine.py:318-345) returns json.load(f) = FLAT {order_id: record}; live orders_state.json has NO top-level 'orders' key (191 entries keyed by order_id), so postpone_sweeper.py:103 orders_state.get('orders', {}) is always {} -> current always {}. (2) records carry 'courier_id' not 'cid' (verified: 'cid' in rec == False, 'courier_id' present), so line 104 current.get('cid') is always None and the POSTPONE_RESOLVED branch (105-109) is unreachable: a double bug. (3) git blame confirms partial fix: line 102 fixed in 4c3c1407 (2026-06-15) while 103-105 still date to b78e31d3 (2026-05-14). (4) Downstream skews real: line 122 current.get('raw') always None; line 128 'if not current' always True -> entries without order_event always DROP_STALE instead of retry. Not an always-propose sentinel, not display-only, not tier-aware R6 confusion. Liveness: dispatch-postpone-sweeper.timer fires every minute and runs the module (verified active, ran 42s ago), so the bug is genuinely wired/executing; dormant only because postponed_proposals.json is {} (sole writer = telegram POSTPONE callback telegram_approver.py:3910-3923, telegram intentionally muted). Latent correctness bug that activates on postpone re-enable -> phantom re-proposal or wrong KOORD escalation for already-assigned orders. P2 confirmed (latent, self-limited by MAX_POSTPONE_COUNT=2, currently empty data).

title: pending_proposals.json entries are only removed by 30-min TTL sweep, never on assignment — relies on every consumer cross-checking orders_state status
evidence: pending_proposals_store removes entries only via sweep_expired (TTL DEFAULT_TTL_SEC=1800, called inside upsert_proposals which itself only runs when shadow emits a PROPOSE). The store docstring (line 16) claims 'panel_watcher usuwa wpis po przypisaniu (pop on ASSIGN)', but panel_watcher only OPENS pending_proposals.json with mode 'r' (lines 221/385/451) — it never writes/pops it. So an order that gets assigned lingers in pending_proposals.json for up to 30 min. The live consumer that matters, pending_global_resweep, guards correctly: line 281 `if not rec or rec.get('status') != 'planned': continue` — it cross-checks current orders_state status and skips assigned orders.
impact: Low: the one active consumer (resweep) filters by live status so stale assigned entries are harmless there. Risk is latent for any FUTURE consumer that treats pending_proposals.json as ground-truth 'currently pending' without re-checking orders_state status — it would act on orders assigned up to 30 min ago.
protocol_note: pending_proposals.json is NOT authoritative for liveness — entries persist up to 30 min after assignment (no pop-on-assign despite the docstring). Any new consumer MUST re-validate rec.get('status')=='planned' against orders_state (as pending_global_resweep.py:281 does). The store docstring claim about panel_watcher popping is stale/false in current code.
recommendation: Treat the docstring's pop-on-assign claim as stale. If a console/overlay consumer is added, enforce the status cross-check pattern from resweep:281.

verification.reasoning: Mechanical claim CONFIRMED on live state: entries are removed only by sweep_expired (TTL=1800s), which runs only inside upsert_proposals (pending_proposals_store.py:64,98-104) and returns early before sweeping when no PROPOSE is collected. Live writer is shadow_dispatcher (ENABLE_PENDING_PROPOSALS_WRITE=true in flags.json, dispatch-shadow active; collects only verdict=='PROPOSE' at line 1289) and never removes an assigned order. The historical pop-on-ASSIGN exists only in telegram_approver (lines 2932/2999/4016/4082/4101), but dispatch-telegram is inactive AND disabled, so no live pop runs. panel_watcher genuinely never writes/pops; all three opens (221/385/451) are mode 'r', so store docstring line 16 ('panel_watcher usuwa wpis po przypisaniu') is inaccurate doc-drift. resweep (tools/pending_global_resweep.py:281) correctly guards on orders_state status != 'planned'. So assigned orders DO linger up to ~30 min. BUT the risk framing is overstated/partial: 3 of 4 current consumers (_check_panel_override, _check_panel_agree with a <=15min sent_at guard, _save_plan_from_pending) fire exactly at the non-duplicate COURIER_ASSIGNED emit, are keyed on the exact assigned order_id, and REQUIRE the entry to still exist at assign-time; popping on assign would BREAK them. The lingering is therefore partly intentional design, not pure oversight, and those consumers do not rely on cross-checking status. The only status-sensitive consumer (resweep) guards correctly. Net residual risk is purely latent-for-future-consumers, which the finding itself acknowledges. P3 (doc inaccuracy + latent footgun) is the correct ceiling; no live correctness bug.
